data.title: Ternium declares $0.18 dividend
data.description:
• Ternium (NYSE:TX) declares  an interim dividend of $1.80 per ADS ($0.18 per share).
  • Payable May 14; for shareholders of record May 9; ex-div May 9.
  • See TX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: AXIL Brands signs licensing agreement with Feld Entertainment for branded hearing protection products
data.description:
• AXIL Brands (OTCQB:AXIL) said on Wednesday that it has entered into a multi-year licensing agreement with Feld Entertainment and Monster Jam, for a Monster Jam-branded hearing protection product portfolio.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, AXIL will produce and sell new adult and youth lines of Monster Jam-branded earbuds, earmuffs, hearing devices, and ear plugs in distribution channels in the United States.
  • The new product lines will feature the signature AXIL sound quality, hearing protection, and enhancement functionality designed for fans of all ages. They are anticipated to be available for purchase in the spring of 2025 at various shops, retail outlets, and online platforms.
  • AXIL Brands (OTCQB:AXIL) shares were up more than 8% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Prologis announces CEO retirement, appointment of a successor
data.description:
• Industrial REIT Prologis (NYSE:PLD) announced on Wednesday the retirement of CEO Hamid Moghadam, and the appointment of President Dan Letter as a successor.
  • PLD shares were trading -2.68% pre-market at $118.06.
  • Co-founder Moghadam is set to retire from the CEO role, effective January 1, 2026. Moghadam will continue as executive chairman.
  • Letter, who has been with Prologis since 2004, will join the company's board immediately.

data.title: Bristol Opdivo + chemo combo shows overall survival benefit in NSCLC
data.description:
• A combination of Bristol Myers Squibb's Opdivo (nivolumab) and platinum-doublet chemotherapy led to a clinically meaningful improvement in overall survival compared to chemo alone in patients with non-small cell lung cancer, according to a new analysis.
  • The final analysis of overall survival in the phase 3 CheckMate -816 study was in patients with resectable NSCLC.
  • Overall survival was a secondary endpoint. The pharma previously reported that primary endpoints, event-free survival and pathological complete response, were also met.

data.title: APi raises FY24 revenue guidance to $7B
data.description:
• APi Group (NYSE:APG) said on Wednesday that the company's net revenues for 2024 will be above its October 31, 2024 guide of about $7B.
  • The company also expects 2024 adjusted EBITDA to be in-line with the midpoint of its guided range of $890 to $900 million. Additionally, the company expects to end the year with a net leverage ratio below our target of 2.5x.
  • Consensus for FY24 revenue is 6.99B.
  • Net revenues for 2025 is expected to range between $7.3B to $7.5B. Meanwhile, adjusted EBITDA is expected to range between $970M to $1B.
  • APi also announced the repricing of its term loan due 2029. The repricing reduces the applicable margin on all outstanding amounts by 25 basis points and represents cash savings of approximately $5 million annually.
  • Additionally, APi said that it repaid $100 million of its term loan due 2029 on December 31, 2024, leaving $2.1B outstanding. APi’s aggregate $1.8B of interest rate swaps related to its term loan remain in effect following the repricing.
  • APG -6.56% premarket to $35.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OGE Energy declares $0.4213 dividend

data.title: Predictive Oncology announces registered direct offering; shares fall
data.description:
• Predictive Oncology (NASDAQ:POAI) has entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of 363,336 shares of common stock at $1.50/share in a registered direct offering.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 19, 2025.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from the offering are expected to be $545,004.
  • POAI intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • POAI shares slump near 16% premarket on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: VanadiumCorp reduces debt by 80% through share issuance for management fees
data.description:
• VanadiumCorp Resource (TSXV:VRB:CA) announced a significant reduction in carried debt from outstanding management fees.
  • The company will be issuing shares for this debt.
  • VanadiumCorp has reached an agreement with eight of ten current and former management and directors to accept shares in exchange for fees owed to them.
  • The parties to the debt settlement have agreed to reduce the original amount of cash owing $458,606 to $91,722; a reduction of 80%.
  • An amount of $258,286 remains owing to former consultants and directors.
  • A total of 733,776 shares will be issued at $0.125.
  • The shares will be subject to a four-month hold period.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Nixxy expects to complete an acquisition by month-end, withdraws BTC financing
data.description:
• Nixxy (NASDAQ:NIXX) said on Wednesday that it has withdrawn a proposed private offering of up to $50M aggregate principal amount of Bitcoin-based, zero-coupon convertible notes.
  • The company continues to evaluate its financing options; however, the board has determined that with Nixxy's current significant progress in its acquisition strategy, combined with less than favorable market conditions, such financing is no longer in the shareholders' best interest.
  • The company's previously discussed business plan to locate an accretive, revenue-generating business or assets for acquisition has come to fruition much more quickly than anticipated.
  • The company is currently concluding an active transaction negotiation with entities that have generated material revenues and show the promise of substantial growth. The company expects, but cannot guarantee, closing an acquisition before the end of February.
  • Nixxy (NASDAQ:NIXX) shares were up more than 9% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Wingstop slides after same-store sales growth misses high-flying expectations
data.description:
Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) fell in early trading on Wednesday after missing sales estimates with its Q4 earnings report. System-wide sales rose 27.6% year-over-year to $1.2 billion during the quarter, helped by 105 net new openings compared to a year ago.

Domestic same-store sales were 10.1% higher year-over-year, against a tough comparable from a year ago of +21.2%. That mark also missed the consensus expectation of +11.8%. Domestic AUV was $2.14 million in Q4, vs. $1.83 million a year ago.

As a percentage of company-owned restaurant sales, cost of sales increased to 77.6% from 75.1% a year ago. The increase was driven by food, beverage and packaging costs, primarily resulting from an increase in the cost of bone-in chicken wings. Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) highlighted that purchases in the prior fiscal fourth quarter were tied primarily to the spot market, which benefited from significant deflation in the cost of bone-in chicken wings.

Adjusted EBITDA increased to $56.4 million from $39.1 million a year ago. GAAP EPS was $0.92 vs. $0.87 consensus and $0.64 a year ago.

There were 2,563 Wingstop restaurants system-wide at the end of Q4. The restaurant tally included 2,204 restaurants in the United States, of which 2,154 were franchised restaurants and 50 were company-owned, and 359 franchised restaurants were in international markets and U.S. territories.

"Due to the strength and staying power of our multi-year strategies, we believe we have line of sight to a new AUV target of $3.0 million," highlighted CEO Michael Skipworth. "This growth in AUVs has further enhanced our best-in-class unit economics, and as we continue to open new restaurants at a record pace, we believe there is an opportunity to more than triple our current U.S. footprint," he added.

Looking ahead, Wingstop (WING) expects low- to mid-single digit domestic same store sales growth in 2025. Global unit growth of 14% to 15% is anticipated.

Shares of Wingstop (WING) were down 7.9% in premarket action to $282.00 vs. the 52-week range of $266.45 to $433.86.
